item.title: Manulife: Regional Director, Specialized Lending, Asian Markets
item.description:
locations
Vancouver, British Columbia
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: April 4, 2025 (13 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25011421
As a trusted partner to advisors, at Manulife Bank, we deliver sophisticated lending solutions and financial strategies that don’t just grow wealth, they amplify it. Built on a foundation of excellence and driven by bold innovation, we empower advisors to deepen relationships, unlock potential, and redefine success for their clients. We’re looking for visionaries who understand where banking is headed; those who can blend the precision of complex financial products with the possibilities of cutting-edge technology. Join us, and be part of a team where banking drives extraordinary outcomes.
It is banking, the way it should be. At Manulife Bank, we believe banking products are an integral part of every Canadian's financial plan. As one of Canada's youngest banks, we are growing at an unprecedented rate and need people with the skills, energy and drive to maintain our leadership position as a "partner's bank." We are currently looking for a Private Lending Specialist – Asian Markets for Vancouver.
The Private Lending Specialist is part of a team of professionals focused on specialized loan and mortgage solutions to Affluent and High Net Worth individuals and their families. The Private Lending Specialist is responsible for developing and growing strong relationships predominately within Manulife Financials existing distribution channels of advisors and centres of influence with a focus on generating Specialized Lending opportunities (primarily Insurance based lending).
Key deliverable for this senior banking role is conducting broad Discoveries for each client; due diligence on loan and mortgage requests including consumer and financial statement analysis; and recommendation for approval of submission.
The successful candidate must be located in Vancouver, BC lower mainland as regular in office visits will be required.
Position Responsibilities:
Ensure flow of business through assessing, packaging and submitting opportunities to the appropriate business unit according to bank standards.
Develop and grow our already strong partnerships with our distribution channels; comprising of approximately 40,000 Investment and Insurance advisors
Collaborate and build partnerships with Bank Account Management, Bank Retail and Insurance Wholesalers
Contribute to the growth and profitability of the territory by maintaining a regular Advisor call program to ensure effective management and expansion of each Advisor relationship. Support Advisors by working directly with centers of influence, professional advisors and end clients
Provide professional presentations to prospective clients as part of an integrated Deal Team
Develop annual, quarterly and monthly business development plans focusing on a clear understanding of goals, run-rates, gaps to plan and key strategies and tactics to close
Management of a pipeline and disciplined approach to focusing on critical activities.
Required Qualifications:
Fluent in English, Mandarin (verbal and in writing)
Expert level of financial analysis of both commercial and personal credit facilities
University Degree or equivalent experience or equivalent industry education
5+ years of experience in Banking including retail and commercial
Preferred Qualifications:
Expert level of financial analysis of both commercial and personal credit facilities
Effective risk management of a banking portfolio including prudent monitoring of overdrafts and credit facilities
CSC and or CFP would be beneficial
"Win-win" oriented negotiator who builds deep relationships with a client and family and is able to deliver difficult messages when necessary
Influence and work collaboratively with banking team in a demanding business environment
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.

item.title: Manulife: Senior Quantitative Analyst
item.description:
locations
Toronto, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: April 4, 2025 (13 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25030160
The Quantitative Analytics team within Financial Risk provides quantitative financial modelling and analytics capability to a variety of stakeholders in Group Risk, Group Finance and Business Units. The team focuses on model, methodology and application development to support liability valuation, risk measurement, insurance product pricing and decision making.
Reporting to the AVP, Quantitative Analytics, the Senior Quantitative Analyst is accountable for the development and implementation of quantitative models to support business growth.

Primary Responsibilities:
Develop sophisticated models to generate economic scenarios for multiple asset classes, enhancing our valuation, pricing, capital, and risk reporting capabilities.
Innovate calibration methodologies and tools for model inputs to ensure accuracy and reliability.
Maintain transparency and facilitate model risk management and audits by meticulously documenting risk models and processes.
Champion continuous improvement initiatives to drive efficiency and effectiveness within the risk modeling function.
Collaborate on ad-hoc projects, providing quantitative expertise to business partners, senior management, and industry working groups.
Stay at the cutting edge of financial modeling by engaging with academic literature and attending workshops and conferences
Required Qualifications:
Master or Ph.D. in Quantitative/Mathematical Finance, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field;
Experience in stochastic modelling with knowledge of financial instruments and risk management principles.
Experience in Python, EXCEL, VBA and SQL, and a passion for leveraging technology to solve complex problems.
Exceptional analytical and problem-solving skills.
Strong communication and documentation proficiency.
Ability to forge strong relationships with business areas and cross-functional teams.
Results-driven with a strong focus on accountability.
Collaborative spirit and the ability to manage multiple stakeholders effectively.

item.title: Manulife: Director Engineering (Insurance)
item.description:
locations
Waterloo, Ontario
Toronto, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: April 18, 2025 (27 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25011347
The opportunity
Are you looking for your next step as an Engineering Leader? We are evolving our Insurance Technology team, with the mandate to power world-class customer and employee experiences through software and engineering skills.
The customer is at the focus of everything we do, and millions of end users rely on our products daily. We believe in the value of empowering engineers with the resources to solve critical problems for the future of the business, which is why we need you. We are seeking a self-motivated Director, obsessed with delivering value, is forward-thinking, and excited to see the successful implementation of the products, frameworks, and patterns delivered.
As a Director, you will:
Lead multi-functional engineering teams, dedicated to building and supporting Insurance Technology systems
Lead your teams to redesign and build client facing technology systems in support of our Insurance and Affinity business in Canada
Assist squads to provide Tier 2 and 3 support under the application criticality framework
Employ re-usable and extensible frameworks, reference implementations and patterns and build new functionality to deliver on key product objectives
Deliver an exceptional user experience, through the delivery of products and services with strong and extendable architectural design
Guide and motivate the development of engineering staff within your team as well as focus on overall team engagement
Maintain and support these services, with accountability for production reliability and usage, and the delivery of new functionality
You bring and will continuously build upon the following skills:
3+ years shown experience leading teams or large scale programs
Preferred Insurance or Banking industry experience
A deep, hands-on, background in modern software development and practices
An entrepreneurial spirit and comfort working within a rapidly changing startup environment; you love the challenge of working on a small team and being part of a larger movement to change the engineering culture of an enterprise
Strong project management experience (planning, estimation, building roadmaps, hiring and building your teams for required deliverables, etc.)
Familiarity with agile and DevOps principles, test-driven development, continuous integration, and other approaches to accelerate the delivery of new features
Developing engineers and a passion for growing staff within the organization
Experimenting with emerging technologies and understanding how they will impact what comes next
A capacity for constant learning from both success and failure, remaining open to change and continuous improvement
Excellent organizational and problem-solving abilities that enable you to manage through creative abrasion
Strong verbal and written communication with the ability to effectively articulate and communicate technical vision, possibilities, and outcomes across all levels in the organization
Understanding of the fundamentals of capital markets or financial services in support of our service or product offerings (or are ready to learn by doing!)
The team works with the following technology:
Languages such as React, NodeJS, AKS and Azure platform
Open source! As we accelerate our delivery of world-class experiences, we know embracing open source technologies will help us get there. Any experience with open source is very attractive to us!
What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.

Our commitment to you
Values-first culture
We lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together.
Boundless opportunity
We create opportunities to learn and grow at every stage of your career.
Continuous innovation
We invite you to help redefine the future of financial services.
Delivering the promise of Diversity, Equity and Inclusion
We foster an inclusive workplace where everyone thrives.
Championing Corporate Citizenship
We build a business that benefits all partners and has a positive social and environmental impact.

item.title: Manulife: Director, Inside Sales
item.description:
locations
Toronto, Ontario
CAN, Quebec, Montreal, 900 Boulevard de Maisonneuve Ouest
Waterloo, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: March 31, 2025 (9 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25021280
As the Director, Inside Sales , you will report into the Head of Retail Distribution, and lead a team of national Inside Wholesaling professionals. We are looking for someone who can lead a high-performing team by establishing a cohesive vision, setting clear job expectations and driving business outcomes from a group of Internal Wholesalers with a high level of engagement. In close collaboration with the Regional Vice Presidents along with our strategic partners (including product, marketing, tax and estate planning), you will seek to drive sales into our Individual Life and Living Benefit Insurance sales and Guaranteed Wealth Solutions.
The role of the Internal Wholesaling team at Manulife focuses on prospecting for new clients (advisors), rekindling relationships with advisors who have stopped supporting Manulife, building relationships with clients who have just begun to do business with us, and partnering with External Wholesalers to cover the top clients in the region. This team also helps to plan and invite clients and prospective clients to Manulife events.
Position Responsibilities:
Actively represent the Inside Sales Team in keeping with the (RDLT) Retail Distribution Leadership Team’s mandate within the organization in order to facilitate a results-based culture.
Establish other partnerships internally with Client Services, Training and Development, Human Resources, Product, Marketing and e-Business, Information Technology, Institutional Sales and Business Analysts.
Implement initiatives in partnership with the RDLT to drive insurance and guaranteed wealth solutions sales.
Contribute to crafting the overall structure of the Internal Wholesaler team (both for the present needs of Manulife and its future needs)
Drive the vision of Inside Sales as aligned with the IIC Distribution Strategy through the creation of transparent performance expectations of Inside Wholesalers (call quality expectations, activity expectations, client coverage expectations, wholesaler partnership expectations, etc.),
Conduct direct management meetings and overall Inside Sales team meetings, drive sales campaigns, craft training and development initiatives and encourage greater involvement and representation on behalf of the organization
Help to set the annual team budget and ensure the team has the tools and training required to carry out their best work while keeping that budget in mind
Required Qualifications:
You have senior-level sales management experience from within the Canadian insurance industry, combined with previous experience (approximately 10 years' worth) as an individual sales rep.
Fully bilingual (French/English): The successful candidate will be required to communicate in English and French in order to support clients from various jurisdictions outside of Quebec.
Preferred Qualifications:
You have excellent relationship management capabilities, combined with strong leadership, social and management skills, with ability to negotiate and influence effectively throughout the organization.
You are a strategic thinker, with a strong affinity for change management and implementation.
You have excellent business insight, ability to communicate, you are a concise decision maker and are deeply effective in your interaction with others.
Demonstrated relationship and leadership skills as well as a consistent track record of sales and operational effectiveness.
You have experience in sales program development, compensation plan modeling, budget management, policy and reporting expertise, systems management, and recruitment initiatives.
You have spent time building and executing Individual Development Plans, setting goals for a team, and measuring performance using metrics.
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.

item.description:

JR25030744
Manulife Investment Management (MIM) Advisor Group delivers financial and investment solutions and advice, including financial planning and insurance, to clients across the wealth spectrum.
The Inside Sales Consultant, being a Transition Consultant, is an important role in the MIM Advisor Group. The Consultant connects with employees in our group plans at Manulife at a time when they are transitioning away from their current employer to understand their investment and other needs so that they may guide and assist them with respect to their plans going forward.
Position Responsibilities:
Conduct one-on-one needs-based conversations over the phone for group retirement plan-members currently enrolled in an employer group plan
Educate and communicate value proposition on benefits of various options for members
Conduct financial illustrations for plan members around drawing retirement income
Guide members through need-based analysis and formulate a plan of action and related suitability for immediate assets that are in group plan
By understanding client preferences and their personal situation, identify referral opportunities
Required Qualifications:
Minimum 1 year in client facing roles in the financial industry, preferability wealth or asset management
Post-Secondary Education is an asset
Life License (LLQP) required by start date
Mutual Fund License (MFDA) is preferred
Certified Financial Planner (CFP) is preferred
Preferred Qualifications:
Demonstrated ability to articulate value proposition through consultation over the phone
Possess active listening skills to understand the clients’ needs and personal situation
Attention to detail in gathering and accurately recording all customer information
High degree of integrity putting the client at the center of the conversation
Strong compliance approach
Remain current and up to date on our products and services, as well as processes
Bilingual (French & English) is required for Quebec applicants only - The successful candidate will be required to communicate in English and French to support clients from various jurisdictions outside of Quebec.

item.title: Manulife: Associate Data Engineer
item.description:
locations
Toronto, Ontario
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: April 6, 2025 (15 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25010936
The Opportunity
Looking for an associate data engineer to join the Canadian data office here at Manulife.
Associate engineer will enable the building, maintenance, enhancements, and support of new and existing Azure data pipelines for various analytical use cases inclusive of AI/ML/BI/GEN AI and reporting. You will partner with team members in understanding data and leveraging it to serve our businesses and operations whilst ensuring adherence to Manulife’s IT security and Risk guidelines.
Responsibilities
Bachelor’s in computer/IT or data related fields required. Master’s degree is a plus.
3+ years of previous data engineering experience, with 2+ years of experience working in enabling azure related data technologies.
Solid understanding of Azure infrastructure; subscriptions, resource groups, resources, access control with RBAC (role-based access control), integrations with Azure AD and Azure security principles (user group, service principal, managed identity), network concepts (VNet, Subnet, NSG rules, private endpoints), password\credential\key management and data protection.
Excellent knowledge of Data viusalization: PowerBI
Strong hands on knowledge of Azure Databricks (Unity Catalog), ADF, ADLS, Synapse Serverless/dedicated/spark pools, Python, PySpark, and T-SQL along with experience designing and developing scripts for ETL processes and automation in Azure data factory and Azure databricks.
High proficiency in GIT/Jenkins/dev ops processes to maintain and resolve issues with data pipelines in prod.
Knowledge of implementing azure technologies and networking via terraform along with ability to fix issues with Azure infra in prod.
Good understanding of data modeling/data marts/Lake house architecture, SCD , data mesh and delta lake overall.
Solid understanding of data privacy and compliance regulations and standard methodologies for preserving customer data.
Knowledge of insurance and financial products is a plus.
What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit.
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile approach and enable business outcomes.
You thrive in teams and enjoy getting things done together.
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters.
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up.
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone.
What we are looking for
Gain deep understanding of data by collaborating with business and advanced analytics partners; demonstrate the understanding to design, curate and publish connected data sets that enables users to self-serve.
Propose technology improvements or come up with innovative solutions to solve business problems.
Collaborate with Architecture, security and risk teams and implement latest guidelines and Azure standard methodologies.
Automate infrastructure provisioning and deployment using tools such as terraform.
Implement CI/CD pipelines that enables automated code deployment.
Participate in Agile sprints and ceremonies; supports rapid iteration and development.
Cultivate and maintain strong relationships and fosters collaboration with various teams and partners within the organization.
What can we offer you?
A competitive salary and benefits packages.
A growth trajectory that extends upward and outward, encouraging you to follow your passions and learn new skills.
A focus on growing your career path with us.
Flexible work policies and strong work-life balance.
Professional development and leadership opportunities.
Our commitment to you
Values-first culture: We lead with our Values every day and bring them to life together.
Boundless opportunity: We create opportunities to learn and grow at every stage of your career.
Continuous innovation: We invite you to help redefine the future of financial services.
Delivering the promise of Diversity, Equity and Inclusion: We foster an inclusive workplace where everyone thrives.

item.title: Manulife: Strategic Relationship Coordinator
item.description:
locations
Boston, Massachusetts
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: April 4, 2025 (13 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25031038
The Opportunity:
Business Development Responsibilities:
Manage projects as assigned by the Strategic Relationship TEAM and ensure the accurate and timely completion of tasks. Projects assigned may include research, correspondence or other work of the department. Independently gather information, test for reasonableness and analyze data. Make recommendations regarding change and solutions to issues or problems.
Independently seek out new learning / development opportunities to enhance job performance and skills.
Participate in internal discussions and feedback sessions around effective best practices for the Strategic Relationship TEAM.
Work with Platform Sales Team Management to coordinate the timely & accurate completion & distribution of quarterly reporting packages.
Relay a positive attitude about department initiatives and stand behind decisions of management.
Through advanced knowledge / use of PowerPoint and Excel, create and maintain broker/dealer meeting presentation decks, business plan updates, and internal presentations.
Coordinate the Strategic Relationship TEAM monthly newsletter.
Event Management Responsibilities:
Work with internal JH Investment Management representatives to plan sales meetings, due diligence meetings, broker/dealer conferences and roadshows.
Support sponsored broker/dealer conferences to include selection of John Hancock representatives, filing appropriate CE, literature, restaurant recommendations, giveaways, and exhibit booths.
Support with preparing for and covering all events, meetings, home office trips, and conferences.
Coordinate logistics for on-site client meetings.
Technology/Analytical Responsibilities:
Ensure daily, weekly and monthly sales reports are accurate and on time.
Generate / create reports for senior management and other support function teams as requested, responsible for accurate and timely reports and providing executive summaries.
Responsible for creating, updating, and maintaining conference salesforce campaigns.
Communicate campaign activity and follow up actions.
Work closely with the TSS on sales, activity, campaign reporting (Power BI).
Act as Salesforce admin, completing CRM needs and requests. Encourage and train to understand and utilize the CRM systems.
Work with Marketing to maintain inventory of marketing literature for home office use using the 4U platform.
Expertise of the following applications: Salesforce, Seismic, Power BI, 4U, all Office applications.
Recommended Experience / Skills:
Bachelor’s Degree required.
Requires broad knowledge of the organization. Work is of a confidential nature, requiring a broad understanding of the company’s business, policies, and practices.
Excellent problem-solving and verbal and written communications skills.
Ability to represent the executive and team to others and to work with other top management.
Proficiency in MS Office applications – Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Access, and software applications unique to the business; Works with standard spreadsheet, database and word processing software packages. Ability to create and run macro commands to more efficiently utilize software packages is preferred.
Flexible and adaptable to rapidly changing priorities and the ability to handle confidential information responsibly and appropriately.
Ability to work with various departments and personalities in a professional and customer focused manner.

item.title: Manulife: Senior Manager / Director, Client Propositions
item.description:
locations
Singapore
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: June 1, 2025 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25031135
The Director, Client Propositions is responsible for the strategic design and introduction of effective propositions to connect client needs to our product solutions. This role is pivotal in significantly scaling our capacity to attract new customers to Manulife whilst enhancing engagement with existing customers, fostering customer loyalty and trust, building stronger, more enduring relationships as their trusted partner at every step of their life journey.
Position Responsibilities:
Spearhead Strategic Client Programmes: Lead the comprehensive lifecycle of our strategic client programmes including conceptualization, cost management, operational execution, technical enhancements, and digital integration to ensure seamless delivery and maximum impact.
Drive Strategic Propositions Development: Collaborate with key business stakeholders to define and refine the strategic propositions for our product solutions targeting specific customer segments, and shape and articulate the customer-centric narrative overarching these propositions.
Design and Lead Implementation of Activation Strategies: Develop a comprehensive and robust activation strategy and own the execution of engagement plans for our client programmes.
Optimize Programme Performance: Establish and execute performance evaluation measures for our programmes, leveraging data-driven insights to provide strategic recommendations for continuous optimization and enhancement.
Oversee Social Media Channels Governance: Manage the governance of our owned social media channels, ensuring adherence to standards and guidelines.
Uphold and Elevate Brand Standards: Ensure adherence to Manulife’s brand standards across all our creative assets, reinforcing our brand identity and focus on customer centricity.
Shape Brand, Content and Propositions Strategy: Drive initiatives that contribute to the overall Brand, Content and Propositions strategy to support Manulife’s ambition of becoming a Digital Customer Leader.
Champion Innovation and Best Practices: Stay abreast of the latest marketing trends, technologies and best practices, and recommend innovative enhancements to ensure our strategies remain highly effective and efficient.
Required Qualifications:
A results-driven professional with a university degree and minimum 10 years experience in financial institutions servicing retail, affluent and HNW clients, experience with insurance preferred.
Demonstrated experience in developing and implementing client propositions for financial institutions.
Strong technical knowledge of financial and insurance concepts.
Demonstrated competency in leading projects/initiatives end-to-end.
Good business acumen with strong strategic thinking and ideation skills.
Familiar with social media channel management and working within governance frameworks.
Possess a positive and agile mindset and excellent stakeholder management ability.
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.

item.description:
locations
Singapore
time type
Part time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: April 30, 2025 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25031241
Manulife is dedicated to fostering a culture of growth, innovation, and excellence. We are seeking a dynamic and detail-oriented Actuarial (Reinsurance) Intern to join our Actuarial team. The Reinsurance Management team resides in the Actuarial Department and is accountable for the overall management of reinsurance arrangements and operations across the organization.
This internship provides an excellent opportunity for hands-on experience, allowing the individual to develop a deep and holistic understanding of reinsurance and how it creates value for the business. The role will also support reinsurance operations and collaborate with various stakeholders in the company as well as our affiliated vendors in regional offices to deliver quality reinsurance arrangements and administration.
Key Responsibilities:
Deliver Reinsurance Reports:
Ensure smooth and timely submissions of reinsurance reports.
Support Implementation:
Assist in the integration of new reinsurance arrangements into the administration system.
Perform Sampling Checks:
Conduct sampling checks on reinsurance transactions to ensure accuracy and compliance.
Individual Accountabilities:
Provide assistance to various reinsurance-related projects and reporting processes.
Coordinate with related stakeholders to facilitate progress and ensure project completion.
Key Shared Accountabilities:
Overseeing the enhancement and development of the reinsurance (RI) administration system. Reviewing and managing in-force reinsurance treaties following best practices. Provide accurate and timely regulatory and management reporting.
Qualifications and Requirements:
Currently enrolled in a Bachelor’s program majoring in Finance, Accounting, or Actuarial Science
Able to commit 10 weeks from May 2025 to July 2025.
Strong prioritization skills and a solution-seeking mindset.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
Proficiency in MS Excel, SQL, and Access.
Why Join Us:
Gain hands-on experience in reinsurance operations and administration.
Collaborate with a diverse team of professionals in a supportive and innovative environment.
Develop a comprehensive understanding of reinsurance and its value to the business.
Opportunity for professional growth and development.

item.title: Manulife: AML Assistant Manager
item.description:
locations
Manila, Manulife Business Processing Services
Cebu City, Cebu
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: April 30, 2025 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25030020
Are you looking for a supportive and collaborative workplace with great benefits and clear career development? You’ve come to the right place.
Why choose Manulife?
Competitive Salary packages and performance bonuses
Day 1 HMO + FREE coverage for your dependents (inclusive of same-sex partners)
Retirement savings benefit
Rewarding culture that values wellness and well-being
Performance Bonus
Global network of industry experts
Extensive training resources
Job Description:
We’re looking for a AML Assistant Manager to join our Integrated Compliance Services team at MBPS. In this role, you will provide oversight in periodic AML/ATF reporting exercises, offer advice on EDD cases from Asia Emerging Markets, oversee Anti-Bribery and Anti-Corruption initiatives—particularly the Gifts and Entertainment Application—and support other general AML programs.
Have the skills and knowledge for the job? Learn more about the opening below!
Key Responsibilities:
Performs assessment of High Net Worth Enhanced Due Diligence cases and provides value adding recommendation to the business on whether to accept or decline high profile clients;
Oversee the Asia Segment monthly and quarterly AML reporting. Review AML reports and provide guidance to the AML/ATF specialist on key reporting tasks;
Participates in the design or modification of reporting requirements and processes, to facilitate the most accurate and efficient data collection;
Collaborate/liaise with Compliance, Operations, and IT personnel in Manulife’s Asia jurisdictions to collect and compile AML/ATF information, on a periodic and also ad-hoc basis.
Handles escalations from Business Units and/or Asia Segment compliance related to General AML Programs
Conducts 1:1 coaching, support, motivation and development of staff
Assists and conducts training needs analysis for personal and professional development of staff
Identify opportunities and process improvement recommendation
Qualifications:
Educational and Professional Background: Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Legal, Finance, Business, or related fields. At least 4-6 years of experience in the Insurance, Asset Management, Banking, or Financial Services sectors, with 1 year of leadership experience. Proven experience in Enhanced Due Diligence review and AML/ATF compliance is highly preferred.
Technical and Industry Knowledge: Familiarity with AML systems and tools such as Bridger and FCRM is advantageous. A strong understanding of financial services and insurance operations and products is a plus.
Leadership and People Management: Demonstrated ability to provide leadership, support, motivation, and development to staff. Experience in managing performance reviews, identifying training needs, and developing strategic approaches for staff development.
Analytical and Decision-Making Skills: Ability to perform assessments of High Net Worth Enhanced Due Diligence cases and provide value-adding recommendations. Skilled in overseeing reporting processes, identifying opportunities for process improvement, and handling escalations related to AML programs.
Communication and Collaboration: Strong written and verbal communication skills, adept at stakeholder management, and team-oriented. Proficiency in MS Office and a detail-oriented, organized approach to work. ACAMS Certification is preferred.
Let's make every day better together. Learn about our opportunities at JOBS.MANULIFE.COM

item.title: Manulife: Digital Paid Media Specialist
item.description:
locations
Hong Kong
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: May 30, 2025 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25020862
The Opportunity
Manulife is embarking on a multi-year journey to build a Global Performance Marketing Shared Service CoE (Center of Excellence) which will fuel our bold ambition to become a digital customer leader. The function brings together Marketing Technology (Martech) and Demand Generation (Demand Gen) – a comprehensive, full-funnel approach designed to drive tailored customer journeys powered by connected data, that when integrated raises brand awareness, drives quality traffic, generates leads, secures sales, and fosters brand loyalty.
Job Description
Reporting into the Senior Manager/Associate Director, Digital Paid Media, Global Digital Marketing, the Digital Paid Media Specialist will support end-to-end delivery for digital marketing campaigns, including digital marketing campaign strategy, set up, execution, optimisation, reporting & insights/analysis. Based in Hong Kong, you will work with a local team of 3, planning & executing a range of full funnel campaigns with both brand and performance marketing objectives.
In this role, you will need to excel in setting up in-platform campaigns, working on media strategies that can be executed with high precision and maximum performance. The successful candidate will support the development of digital media campaigns internally. You will be responsible for smooth campaign execution, so deep knowledge of platform setup, trafficking, tagging, reporting and key campaign-related processes is required for the role.
Position Responsibilities:
Support the development and execution of end-to-end digital media campaign marketing strategy and execution for our Global Wealth and Asset Management and Insurance divisions across a variety of products, ensuring that it links all marketing elements across both paid digital and traditional media.
Work hand in hand with various teams on media operations and goals to make informed decisions around targeting, platforms, creative formats, and budget allocation.
Analyze and apply industry trends, performance data, and other resources to inform strategies, audience development, test-and-learn opportunities, optimizations, and measurement frameworks.
Track, share, and carefully adhere to timelines
Assist with process optimization for enhanced campaign efficiency both within the Hong Kong team as well as the wider global Demand Generation team
Conduct competitor analysis within categories with a focus on Share of Voice (SOV) in SEM and other channels.
Required Qualifications:
2-3+ years of experience working in digital media across all channels in Hong Kong (search, social, programmatic, video, etc.), certified with the latest Google/Meta badges
Media agency background in planning and managing full funnel multi-channel digital media, especially on performance marketing and lead generation.
In depth, hands-on working knowledge of buying ads on both biddable and non-biddable channels, including Google Ads, Facebook Ads, Google Marketing Platform (DV360/CM360/SA360), with an overall understanding of tools such as Adobe Analytics, CDP
Experience in trafficking, tagging journeys and reporting tools like CM360
Ability to pull strong data driven insights and make optimisations based on internal and external environmental factors
Ability to prioritise evolving business / landscape needs and manage different resources in an agile fashion
Set up/trafficking for CID/UTM architecture and implementation
Ability to analyze large datasets across various media platforms to uncover meaningful insights and provide actionable recommendations.
A quick learner with good problem-solving skills who is open to challenges and has a strong growth-mindset and passion for digital media
Financial background or experience working in a highly regulated industry is a nice to have
Excellent presentation and communication skills
Fluent in both written and spoken Cantonese & English
When you join our team:
We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.

job requisition id
JR25010225
UI/UX Designer
Are you looking for a supportive, collaborative workplace with great teams and inspiring leaders? You’ve come to the right place. We’re looking for ambitious people who share our values and want to make every day better for people around the world. If this sounds like you, and the career below sounds exciting, we’d like to hear from you.
The Opportunity
The customer is the focus of everything we do, and millions of end users rely on our products daily. We believe in the value of empowering with the resources to solve critical problems for the future of our business, which is why we need you.
What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile mindset and enable business outcomes
You thrive in teams, and enjoy getting things done together
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone
We are looking for someone with:
3 - 5 years of dedicated UX design experience designing complex solutions for complete digital environments.
Bachelor’s degree in design, Psychology, Human-factors or similar discipline.
In-depth knowledge and experience of user interface design with consumer applications, preferably mobile devices, rich web apps/site, interactive TV within the media, telecommunications and broadband industries.
Outstanding portfolio of previous work of digital/mobile products, interfaces, large platform design.
Extensive knowledge and experience with driving and applying user-centered design processes while working collaboratively with customers, along with cross-functional teams including engineering, product management and user research.
Expertise in UX design best practices, service design, testing methodologies, and usability and accessibility concerns.
Expertise in UX software such as Figma, Adobe Creative Cloud, Basic HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript skills are a plus.
A strategic perspective on the variety of work that we do. Many of our projects involve strategic thinking based upon the insights gathered from the field. Thus, business, brand, and/or strategic experience are desirable.
A natural skill to engage with people at a deep level. Whether observing or interviewing customers, or working with fellow team members and clients, a service designer should be passionate about representing the human perspective in design and should have skills in bringing this passion to life for the team.
Ability to clearly and effectively articulate design processes, ideas, and solutions to teams and clients
Ability to work independently on a project level and identify and solve problems effectively and efficiently, as well as collaborate and communicate closely with teams and other stakeholders regularly present design solutions for approval.
A deep understanding of and passion for championing the importance of human centered design across the company.
Passion for helping teammates, sharing knowledge and experience, and learning from them.
Openness to receiving feedback and constructive criticism.
Excellent and effective communication and persuasive skills are required.
Excellent command of spoken and written English.
Nice to Haves:
Ability to build and manage relationships with the key internal stakeholders
On the job you will:
Be a storyteller – A critical element of the role is to deliver insights about people and behavior – verbally and visually in a way that generates empathy, emotion, and engagement from the client and design team.
Facilitate client’s problem statement definition by validating product assumptions, advocating for the customer’s perspective, and considering the business objectives.
Support with research to understand the business, our customer and agents. Create maps, personas and other artefacts to aid understanding and communication.
Work alongside researchers to plan and run customers and agent research, such as observational studies, customer interviews, usability testing, and other forms of qualitative and quantitative research to uncover insights, learn about user behavior and verify design concepts.
Take smart risks and champion new ideas.
Identify design problems through in-depth analysis of research and ideate elegant solutions and customer experience.
Translate concepts into wireframes and mockups that lead to intuitive user experiences, optimized for a wide range of devices and interfaces.
Rapidly test and iterate your designs, using storytelling and rapid prototyping methods, ranging from paper sketching to digital prototyping.
Collaborate with other team members and stakeholders and bring them along at every step of the design process.
Work closely with development teams to ensure that design specifications are implemented.
Participate as a contributor to an interdisciplinary team that includes other designers, researchers, project management, business and brand strategists, and hardware and software developer.
Our commitment to you
Our mission; to be a part of making Decisions Easier and Lives Better
A leadership team dedicated to your growth and success
A bold ambition and set of goals to be a leader in driving transformation in our industry
Our best. Every day.
Learn more about opportunities with us at jobs.manulife.com
Every career at Manulife provides the opportunity to learn new skills and move your career forward. Ready to make an impact somewhere? What are you waiting for? Apply today!
About Manulife and John Hancock
Manulife Financial Corporation is a leading international financial services provider, helping people make their decisions easier and lives better. To learn more about us, visit https://www.manulife.com/en/about/our-story.html.

job requisition id
JR25030475
Are you ready to take on a role that offers unlimited opportunities for growth and success? We’re seeking a proactive Manager for Analytics, Automation & Control Assurance Testing to enhance our risk management frameworks and ensure our operations meet rigorous internal and external standards.
Why Choose Our Company?
Competitive salary packages and performance bonuses
Day 1 HMO + FREE coverage for your dependents (inclusive of same-sex partners)
Retirement savings benefit
Rewarding culture that values wellness and well-being
Global network of industry experts
Extensive training resources
About the Role:
We are seeking a dynamic and innovative professional with expertise in both Analytics & Automation and Control Assurance Testing. This role requires a strategic mindset, strong leadership, and a deep understanding of risk management, data analytics, and automation. The Manager will be responsible for executing and providing objective assessments of risk management activities, developing automation strategies, and enhancing control assurance processes.
Key Responsibilities:
Execute assurance projects recognizing inherent, control, and regulatory risks.
Ensure assurance procedures align with Global IRM Methodologies.
Conduct risk assessments of internal controls and operating practices.
Manage multiple assurance projects to meet time and quality objectives.
Track and report on management corrective action plans.
Participate in assurance timeline planning and stay updated with internal and external drivers.
Qualifications:
Experience: Minimum 8 years in Assurance and Control testing, Information Security, Business Resiliency, or Technology Risk within a large enterprise.
Skills: Proven experience in IT controls assurance data analytics and automation delivery, understanding of data analytics ecosystem components, and previous audit or 2nd line oversight experience.
Education: University degree in Computer Science or a related discipline.
Certifications: Professional designations such as CA/CPA, CISSP, CISA, CRISC, FAIR, MBCP.
What You'll Gain:
In-depth knowledge of global risk management practices.
Opportunities to shape strategic initiatives and enhance your professional profile.
Exposure to a dynamic and collaborative work environment.
If you're a detail-oriented leader with a passion for risk management and innovation, we’d love to hear from you!

item.description:
locations
Quezon City
Lapu Lapu City,
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: March 31, 2025 (8 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24090084
Are you looking for a supportive and collaborative workplace with great benefits and clear career development? You’ve come to the right place.
Why choose Manulife?
Competitive Salary packages and performance bonuses
Day 1 HMO + FREE coverage for your dependents (inclusive of same-sex partners)
Retirement savings benefit
Rewarding culture that values wellness and well-being
Performance Bonus
Global network of industry experts
Extensive training resources
Job Description:
We’re looking for a Compliance Specialist to join our team at MBPS. In this role, you are expected to support AML/ATF and programs administered by the Global CAMLO Office.
Have the skills and knowledge for the job? Learn more about the opening below!
Key Responsibilities:
Perform AML/ATF (Anti-Money Laundering/Anti-Terrorist Financing) and sanctions name screening to support the onboarding and transaction compliance approval process for Group Functions
Provide support in AML/ATF related regulatory reporting (e.g. OSFI 525/590)
Coordinate the collection of monthly sanctions metrics and collate results for regulatory reporting
Provide support in the annual risk and controls assessment process
Maintaining the country risk list through monthly and ad hoc research, updates, publishing and communications to all segments
This job description does not represent a comprehensive listing of job duties that are required of the employee performing this role. We reserve the right to change duties or assign additional duties at any time with or without notice.
Qualifications:
Experience in Compliance and Financial Services: 4-6 years of experience in the financial services sector, preferably in compliance (AML/ATF, sanctions, or audit), is essential. Experience with regulatory reporting, analytics, and familiarity with compliance technology (e.g., Bridger/FCRM) is preferred.
Educational Background and Certifications: A bachelor's degree in a relevant field such as Finance, Business, or Legal is required. A professional certification in Compliance or AML, such as CAMS, is an advantage.
Technical Proficiency: Proficiency with Microsoft productivity tools such as Excel, Word and PowerPoint is necessary. Experience with generative AI and prompting, as well as metrics consolidation and analysis, is preferred.
Analytical and Risk Assessment Skills: Knowledge of testing and risk assessment processes is advantageous. The ability to independently conduct analysis and testing, including watchlist regression testing and key risk indicator reporting, is important.
Communication and Documentation Skills: Fluent English reading and writing skills are required. Strong communication skills are needed for reporting, vendor relationship management, and responding to compliance-related inquiries. Experience in process documentation is beneficial.

item.description:

Lapu Lapu City,

JR25030763
Are you looking for a supportive and collaborative workplace with great benefits and clear career development? You’ve come to the right place.
Why choose Manulife?
Competitive Salary packages and performance bonuses.
Day 1 HMO + FREE coverage for your dependents (inclusive of same-sex partners).
Retirement savings benefit Rewarding culture that values wellness and well-being .
Performance Bonus Global network of industry experts.
Extensive training resources.
Job Description:
We’re looking for a Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) Analyst to join our Integrated Compliance Services team at MBPS. In this role, you are responsible in conducting enhanced due diligence investigations consistent with the Company AML policy and EDD procedures. Prepare and complete the EDD template for business unit review, including accurate narratives that will help the business make risk-based decisions on potential applicants and distributors.
Key Responsibilities:
Conduct background investigations as required and coordinate with Regional Compliance or local compliance regarding red flags found on High Net-worth individuals, Keyman insurance, and PICs.
Communicate the case status with the Business Unit and Regional Compliance and any potential AML/ATF red flags.
Initiate knowledge sharing to the enhanced due diligence team and Global Investigative Forensic Services for any unusual/unique findings encountered during research.
Ensure that tracking tools are updated with the correct information necessary to track monthly EDD data.
Proactive participation with other segments through completing adhocs or any additional EDD requests.
Qualifications:
Education: Must be Bachelors Degree holder preferably in Economics, Banking, Legal or Business area.
Schedule: Morning Shift 8:00AM-5:00PM or 9:00AM-6:00PM
Work Arrangement: Hybrid. Amenability and readiness to work onsite and from home anytime (dependent on business need AND/OR current external environment/situation).
Nice to have skills:
1 year of relevant experience with enhanced due diligence or AML/ATF work exposure.
Exposure to life insurance and investment products for at least 1 year. Understanding of OECD FATCA (Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development, Foreign Account Tax Compliance Act), CRS (Common reporting standards) and other AML regulatory framework would be an asset.
Effectively apply technical skills (i.e. foreign language, Boolean methodology, data analysis, etc.) and soft skills (i.e. critical thinking, problem-solving, information analysis and decision-making) in conducting EDD.
Research and Writing.
Knowledge of the wide-ranging and complex variety of fund/corporate structures (e.g. UBO) and ACAMS certification is an advantage.

The customer is the focus of everything we do, and millions of end users rely on our products daily. We believe in the value of empowering Specialist, Finance with the resources to solve critical problems for the future of our business, which is why we need you. 
The successful incumbent is responsible to conducting moderately complex financial, accounting and economic analyses, including journal entry preparation, taxation, treasury, payments and internal and external reporting.

You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit

Join our Finance Team in Phnom Penh now!
The Opportunity
The customer is the focus of everything we do, and millions of end users rely on our products daily. We believe in the value of empowering Senior Specialist, Finance with the resources to solve critical problems for the future of our business, which is why we need you. 
The successful incumbent is responsible to conducting moderately complex financial, accounting and economic analyses, including journal entry preparation, taxation, treasury, payments and internal and external reporting.
What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit 
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile mindset and enable business outcomes 
You thrive in teams, and enjoy getting things done together
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters 
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone
We are looking for someone with: 
Bachelor’s degree in finance/ accounting, professional qualification (ACCA, CPA) is a plus
At least 3 to 4 years of relevant working experience in finance, accounting and taxation. Candidates with experience in banking or insurance companies will be of added advantage
Basic knowledge in Accounting and Taxation and operational systems, knowledge of the Sun Accounting System will be of added advantage
Nice to Haves: 
Knowledge of IFRS will be of added advantage
Proficient in Microsoft Excel, Word is required
Self-motivated, able to work independently, possess good inter-personal communication skill both internally and externally
High comfort level working in a diverse environment and a dynamic team player
On the job you will: 
Involve in monthly tax and annual Tax on Income preparation and declaration to GDT including E-filling
Involve in the daily Treasury Management and Relationship with Banking and financial institutions
Involve in review and processing payment as assigned by the manager
Prepare/ post journal entries, perform reconciliations, assist in the month-end closing of the accounts
Coordinate with external tax consultant if any
Review the reconciliations and investigation on accounting issues
Assist in preparation the financial reporting to both internal and external stakeholders
Complete assigned tasks in a timely manner
Participate in departmental special projects and perform other duties as assigned
Our commitment to you  
Our mission; to be a part of making Decisions Easier and Lives Better

A bold ambition and set of goals to be a leader in driving transformation in our industry 
Our best. Every day.

item.title: Manulife: Business Relationship Manager
item.description:
locations
Battambang
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: May 3, 2025 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR25031225
Job Posting Description
The Business Relationship Manager is responsible for providing life insurance solutions to Bank's clients. He/she will work in coordination with branch sales and service teams; thus, we are looking for a dynamic individual, who has an independent mindset and the drive to succeed.
What motivates you?
You obsess about customers, listen, engage and act for their benefit
You think big, with curiosity to discover ways to use your agile mindset and enable business outcomes
You thrive in teams, and enjoy getting things done together
You take ownership and build solutions, focusing on what matters
You do what is right, work with integrity and speak up
You share your humanity, helping us build a diverse and inclusive work environment for everyone
We Are Looking For Someone With:
Interpersonal Skills: Strong relationship-building and communication skills to effectively interact with bank staff and customers.
Sales Acumen: Proficiency in sales techniques and the ability to drive sales performance.
Training Ability: Capability to conduct effective training sessions and improve the product knowledge of bank employees.
Customer Orientation: A focus on providing excellent customer service and addressing customer needs.
Analytical Skills: Ability to analyze market trends, sales data, and feedback to inform business strategies.
Problem-Solving: Aptitude for resolving issues and managing conflicts between Manulife and the bank partner, if any.
On The Job You Will:
Relationship Management:
Act as the primary point of contact between the bank partner and Manulife, ensuring smooth communication and coordination.
Build and maintain strong, productive relationships with bank staff, including branch managers, and other key stakeholders.
Sales Support and Training:
Assist bank staff in understanding and selling insurance products. Provide expertise and support during customer interactions, if required.
Conduct regular training sessions for bank employees on insurance products, sales techniques, and regulatory requirements.
Business Development:
Ensure that licensed bank employees are knowledgeable about the features, benefits, and competitive advantages of the insurance products offered.
Collaborate with bank branches to achieve sales targets for insurance products. Develop strategies and action plans to drive sales growth.
Design and monitor marketing and sales campaigns in coordination with both bank partner and Manulife Banca S&P teams.
Identify opportunities for cross-selling and up-selling insurance products to the bank partner’s existing customer base.
Customer Service:
Handle customer queries and issues related to insurance products, ensuring a high level of customer satisfaction.
Assist in policy servicing activities, such as policy renewals, claims processing, and addressing customer complaints.
Market Intelligence:
Observe market trends and competitor activities to provide insights and recommendations for product development and sales activity improvements.
Gather feedback from bank staff and customers to help improve product offerings and service delivery.
Compliance and Reporting:
Ensure that all sales and marketing activities comply with relevant regulatory requirements and company policies.
Prepare and submit regular reports on sales performance, market trends, and relationship status to both Manulife and the bank partner.
Work closely with branch sales and service teams to provide innovative product solutions to address client's protection, and long-term saving needs
Strengthen the long-term relationships with clients by providing excellent customer service
Perform other tasks as required.
Our commitment to you:
Our mission; to be a part of making Decisions Easier and Lives Better
A leadership team dedicated to your growth and success
A bold ambition and set of goals to be a leader in driving transformation in our industry
Our best. Every day.
We are committed to be an equal opportunity employer and welcome applications from all suitably qualified persons regardless of their race, sex, disability, religion/belief, sexual orientation or age”.

item.title: Manulife: Actuarial Analyst
item.description:
locations
Quezon City
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: May 31, 2025 (30+ days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR24051774
Are you looking for a supportive and collaborative workplace with great benefits and clear career development? You’ve come to the right place.
Why Join MBPS Actuarial?
Since its establishment in 2012, the MBPS Actuarial team has grown into Manulife's Actuarial Center of Excellence and has become the country's largest Actuarial employer. Their unwavering dedication to precision and ability to distill complex data into actionable insights has allowed them to continuously set the bar higher with their industry-defining expertise in Valuation, Liability and Asset Modelling, Experience Analytics, Pricing, and more.
MBPS Actuarial offers a wide range of global opportunities across multiple actuarial disciplines, a high performing culture, a market leading actuarial development program, a job rotation program, and a leadership team dedicated to your growth and success. Beyond that, their influence extends to the broader community and industry, enriching the professional landscape for everyone involved.
Job Description:
We’re looking for a talented and driven Actuarial Analyst to join our Actuarial Function at MBPS. In this role, you will work closely with our Actuarial team to collect, analyze, and interpret data that drive key business decisions. With our team’s long-established history of success, you will be embark on a rewarding and engaging career path that exposes you to different facets of our global organization.
Have the skills and knowledge for the job? Learn more about the opening below!
NOTE: The final job title and level you receive will be dependent on your years of experience and your interviews during the application process.
Key Responsibilities:
Collaborate with our skilled team of actuaries to support the company’s strategic data analysis, risk assessment, and business planning
Support the business in tasks that may include the following:
Pricing
Portfolio Modeling
Asset Modeling
Valuation
Collaborate cross-functionally to ensure precise financial projections, optimal pricing strategies, and effective risk-management solutions
Drive innovation by staying at the forefront of industry trends, regulatory shifts, and technological advancements
Qualifications:
Bachelor’s Degree in Actuarial Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Finance or related field
At least 1 year of actuarial work experience in Insurance or Financial Services. Fresh graduates are welcome to apply and will be considered for the Actuarial Assistant role.
Progress towards attaining a designation in any recognized actuarial organization (SOA, ASP)
Working experience in MS Office (Excel, VBA, Access), GGY AXIS, SQL, Qlikview, Python or similar program is a plus
Proven analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to gather, synthesize, organize, and report information, results, and recommendations to various levels across the organization
Strong ability to establish priorities and manage multiple tasks with accuracy and attention to detail
Able to work in an evolving and dynamic environment
Strong communication skills with the ability to build camaraderie across levels and business functions
Passion to grow and driven to be recognized as a trusted business partner.
Ready to join our high-performing team of Actuarial experts? Apply today!
